remarks: Door fetched up, hauled back early
Long line gear attached to port wing.
set_result: 2 - NORMAL - Minor damage to gear - Catch unaffected

remarks: Cartier to stbd.  Tear up stbd wing on Teleost.  Cartier also had a tear up but on port wing.  Repairing trawl and plan to re-tow for both vessels
Cartier to port. Trawl came fast on bottom, hauled back early.  No damage.  Cartier also hauled early and had damage to bellies.  Moving on from this area.
Cartier on stbd. Crew discovered damage to stbd wing after sampling had begun. Stbd wing replaced. Already moved on to next station by time damage was identified.
Cartier to stbd. Lost headline sensor @ 15 min mark and did not come back on.

Cartier to port. Door sensors coming in and out. Changed height sensor but no reading. Depth sensor reading ~3.5m above bottom which is the expected height/opening. Strong tide. 20 min tow due to uncertainty with sensors - although still large catch.
Cartier to stbd. Bad bottom all around station. Moved tow 3 miles south from station to complete a comparative tow on better bottom
Cartier on stbd. Hauled back at 23 min due to bad bottom.
Alone. Lobster pot wedged in bunt blocking codend. May have effected catch so considered bad tow. Additional station in stratum to attempt if time permits before Cartier rejoins.
Cartier on port. Port door fetched up momentarily at 8 min.
Cartier to stbd. Lobster pot in cod end
set_result: 2 - NORMAL - Minor damage to gear - Catch unaffected

remarks: Cartier to port.  Plankton tow planned for station 192 will be conducted by Cartier on station 190
Cartier on stbd. Lobster trap caught in trawl. May have affected catch.
Re-tow. Cartier was on stbd. Came fast on bottom at 23 min on stbd side.  Hauled back and no gear damage.
Cartier ahead. Tow track crossed stratum line and re-entered correct stratum, but depth stayed within stratum depth for full duration of tow.

Cartier to port. Lobster pot in cod end.  Sampled the set but later learned that there was a rip in the liner as well.  Likely will have to be deemed unusable for comparative as fish could have escaped

Cartier to stbd. Sensor issues for us on tow.  Came fast at 25 minutes.  Height, wing and door sensors were not working properly for most of the tow.  Trawl came back with footgear flipped over.  May have happened when came fast, but not sure.  Considering a null set,  moving to an a secondary location approx 3 miles to the south of this station.
NO WING OR DOOR SENSORS FOR DURATION OF TOW
Cartier on stbd side. Some longline hauled up just in cod end.
Cartier on STBD side, 0.5nm distance not met due to desired depth.
Cartier on stbd side, Cartier height sensor malfunction, Cartier aborted their initial tow, re-tow within 1hr.
Cartier on Port side. Haul back early due to bad bottom.
Cartier on Port side. Null tow, significant tear up. Damage to bellies and codend.
Cartier on Port Side, Fishing gear on Station
Cartier on stbd side. Tow ended early due to bad bottom.
Cartier on stbd side, offset 0.5nm ahead. Short tow due to lack of bottom.
No comparative this set.  Only enough bottom for 1 tow

Cartier on Stbd Side
Took out bottom belly of trawl, set null
Cartier on Stbd Side. Same side as last tow in order to get both full tows in, only enough bottom for 20min in one track so that was left for the Cartier.
Cartier 2 cables to port due to water depth and hazards in area.  Cartier started tow 10 minutes after ours to allow stagger.  Tow cut short due to vessel traffic.
Hydraulic issues with main trawl winches on first attempt.  Restarted Tow ~40min after Cartier.  Cartier on Stbd side

Cartier on Port Side. Height sensor began actin erratically during tow as though it was flipped over at times. All other sensors were normal, doors and bridles showed good contact and nothing was unusual about the catch. Tow was judged as successful. BME
Cartier did not complete comparative tow due to issues with height sensor. Two Cartier attempts aborted.
Cartier on stbd side. Take back early for upcoming bad bottom.
Cartier on port side. Haul back early due to NAFO Zone limitations.
Cartier on port side. Non-random tow for comparative purposes only.  Changed stratum from 443 to 444 based on depth of realized tow - EM
Cartier to stbd and behind 10 minutes
Cartier on Stbd side, 8 second lag in end time corrected. BME
Start tow is about 45 seconds behind due to lag in system. Andes was slow to respond to entries so the winch control system was used to time the set.  Tow time was 30 minutes taken from Aldebaran.
